stimulus: In traditional ensemble jazz, soloists generally avoid stepping outside the agreed harmonic ______ violate that framework risks unsettling the rhythm section and disrupting the call-and-response between musicians on stage.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The period correctly marks the boundary between one complete sentence ("In traditional…structure") and another ("To violate…stage"), with the infinitive phrase "To violate that framework" functioning as the subject of the second clause.

Choice A is incorrect because it produces a comma splice between two independent clauses. Choice B is incorrect because it creates a run-on sentence: the two clauses are fused without punctuation or a conjunction. Choice D is incorrect because adding "As" before "to" produces an awkward sentence fragment beginning with the conjunction "as," which has no main clause to attach to in the second sentence.
